zh.Near Ambion Hill, south of Market Boswor: 英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin是Heilyn ap Einion Sais和Angharad ferch Hywel的兒子，於大約1445年出生在威爾士安格萊波多爾根。他於1478年與Angharad ferch Gwilym or William在威爾士結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國威爾斯。

zh-hans.Near Ambion Hill, south of Market Boswor: 英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin是Heilyn ap Einion Sais和Angharad ferch Hywel的儿子，于大约1445年出生在威尔士安格萊波多爾根。他于1478年与Angharad ferch Gwilym or William在威尔士结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世在他的家乡，埋葬在英国威尔斯。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Heylin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Longford and Leitrim): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEislin, a shortened form of the older Ó hEisleanáin ‘descendant of Eisleanán’, a byname probably derived from éislinn ‘unsafe, weak’.

English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Alselin, from ancient Germanic Anselin, with assimilatory change of n to the following -l-. This is a French form of ancient Germanic Ans(e)helm, composed of the elements ans- ‘(pagan) god’ and helm- ‘helmet’; compare Anselm .
English: topographic name for a dweller ‘(by the) hazel copse’, from Old English hæsling, or a habitational name from any of various minor places called with this word.

variants.name: Henslin
meanings: Irish: possibly a variant of   Heslin  .
South German: from a pet form of   Hensel   (see   Hans  ).

variants.name: Anselm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Heinz.

English and German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Anshelm, composed of the elements ans- ‘god’ + helm ‘protection, helmet’. The personal name was taken to France and England by St. Anselm (c. 1033–1109), known as the Father of Scholasticism. He was born in Aosta, Italy, joined the Benedictine order at Bec in Normandy, France, and in 1093 became archbishop of Canterbury, England.

nameMeanings.name: Llewellyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

variants.name: Llywellyn
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin was born about 1445, in Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales as the son of Heilyn ap Einion Sais and Angharad ferch Hywel. He married Angharad ferch Gwilym or William in 1478, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died in his hometown, and was buried in Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin est né environ 1445, à Bodorgan, Anglesey, Galles, fils de Heilyn ap Einion Sais et Angharad ferch Hywel. Il a épousé Angharad ferch Gwilym or William en 1478, à Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin wurde um 1445 in Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales, als Sohn von Heilyn ap Einion Sais und Angharad ferch Hywel geboren. Er hat 1478 in Wales Angharad ferch Gwilym or William geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Wales, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin è nato il circa 1445 nel Bodorgan, Anglesey, Galles come figlio di Heilyn ap Einion Sais e Angharad ferch Hywel. Lui sposato Angharad ferch Gwilym or William nel 1478, nel Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Galles, Regno Unito.

ja.Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales: ウェールズアングレセイボドルガン
ja.Near Ambion Hill, south of Market Boswor: イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルス
ja.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylinは約1445年にウェールズアングレセイボドルガンでHeilyn ap Einion SaisとAngharad ferch Hywelの息子として生まれた。1478年にウェールズでAngharad ferch Gwilym or Williamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は故郷で死去し，イギリスウェールズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin(은)는 대략 1445년 웨일스 앤글레시 보도르건에서 Heilyn ap Einion Sais(와)과 Angharad ferch Hywel의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1478년 웨일스에서 Angharad ferch Gwilym or William(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 웨일스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin nasceu aproximadamente 1445, em Bodorgan, Anglesey, Gales filho de Heilyn ap Einion Sais e Angharad ferch Hywel. Ele casou-se com Angharad ferch Gwilym or William em 1478, em Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Gales, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin родился около 1445 г. в местности бодорган, англеси, Уэльс. Его родителей звали Heilyn ap Einion Sais и Angharad ferch Hywel. Он женился на женщине по имени Angharad ferch Gwilym or William в 1478 г. в местности Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности Уэльс, Великобритания.

sm.from 1450 to 1649: mai le 1450 e o'o I le 1649

es.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Heylin nació aproximadamente 1445, en Bodorgan, Isla Oscura, Gales como hijo de Heilyn ap Einion Sais y Angharad ferch Hywel. Se casó con Angharad ferch Gwilym or William en 1478, en Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Gales, Reino Unido.
